critical_issues: Four features (F-057 Global Search, F-066 Branding Config, F-071 Demo Environment, F-075 Data Import) are marked as MVP in their registry entries but are absent from the cross-reference manifest's mvp_features list AND absent from every post_mvp tier list (growth, expansion, future). Downstream phases consuming the manifest will not find these features in any tier, risking them being omitted from Phase 4 data architecture, Phase 7 build cards, etc.

F-087 (Proactive Seasonal Outreach Automation) has conflicting tier assignments: marked 'Growth' in its registry heading, appears in Growth roadmap Batch 4 (Stage 2), but is listed under 'future' in the cross-reference manifest's post_mvp_features. Phase 7 build sequencing cannot determine when to schedule this feature.

Feature Registry Summary by Tier table claims Future = 17, but the module-level breakdown within the same row (Multi-Operator 5 + Membership 1 + Corporate 1 + Intelligence 1 + Help 1 + Outreach 2 + Data 1 + Network 1 + Portal 1) sums to 14, not 17. Three features are unaccounted for in the breakdown, making the tier total unverifiable.
contradictions: D3 sets membership launch at 'spring 2027' (~6 months post-launch, per D55-P3-001 and Expansion stage milestone), but D1 Section 4.2 Stage 3 defines the membership trigger as 'a client base large enough (500+ unique clients served)'. At projected ~180 clients/year, 500+ unique clients wouldn't be reached until roughly 18+ months post-launch. The new decision does not explicitly acknowledge or override D1's trigger condition.

F-028 (Seasonal Pricing Management) is explicitly described as '*Subsumed within F-025 (Course Profiles)*' in its registry entry, yet it appears as a separate line item in the Section 3.3 MVP Feature List table and is counted as one of the 33 MVP features. The introductory note states subsumed capabilities are 'not counted as separate entries,' directly contradicting the actual counting.

Growth roadmap Batch 4 (Section 4, Stage 2) lists 'F-057–F-059: Filtered views, saved filters, quick-access recent records' as a Growth batch, but F-057 (Global Contextual Search) is marked MVP in the Feature Registry (Module 9). An MVP feature cannot appear in a Growth build batch.

D3's roadmap compresses D1's growth stages substantially (D3 Growth = months 2-4 vs. D1 Stage 2 = months 6-18; D3 Expansion = months 5-12 vs. D1 Stage 3 = months 18-30; D3 Future = 12+ months vs. D1 Stage 4 = 30-42 months) without explicitly acknowledging the divergence from D1's timeline or explaining why the acceleration is justified beyond the general AI-speed argument.
warnings: The MVP Dependency Verification table (Section 2.3) claims 'Every MVP feature has its dependencies also in MVP. Verified:' but only lists 20 of the 28+ MVP features that have dependencies. Eight features with dependencies are missing from the table: F-015, F-016, F-022, F-024, F-026, F-027, F-028, F-038. All would pass verification, but the 'verified' claim is incomplete.

F-031 (Branded Stripe Payment Links) lists only F-001 as a dependency in its feature description, but the dependency chain diagram in Section 2.2 shows F-031 depending on both F-001 and F-005 (Pricing Calculation). The verification table also only shows F-001. Since F-031 needs calculated pricing to generate correct payment amounts, F-005 appears to be a genuine missing dependency.

The Stripe cross-border base rate in Section 5.1 uses 2.9% (domestic card rate), while D1 Section 6.3 specifies 3.1% for international cards from Stripe Mexico. Since payments are from US cards to a Mexican business, D1's 3.1% international rate is more precise. The stated 'approximately 4.5-5%' range covers both, but the component breakdown is inaccurate.

F-057 (Global Contextual Search) is categorized as 'infrastructure, not counted as discrete product features' in Section 3.3, but it is a genuine user-facing feature with its own UI, complexity rating (High), and role-based access. Classifying it as non-discrete infrastructure while it has a full registry entry with an F-ID creates confusion about its status.

D3 Expansion stage announces 'Ocean Golf Preferred membership launches' as a milestone, but the membership management feature (F-084) is tiered as Future (12+ months), not Expansion (months 5-12). The document does not clarify how a membership program launches without its management feature, or whether 'launches' means a manual/informal launch using existing features.

The manifest's post_mvp_features.future list contains 13 features, not the 17 claimed in the tier summary table. Combined with the 4 orphaned MVP-marked features missing from all manifest tier lists, the manifest's feature inventory does not account for all 89 features.
